methodsAttempted.query: site:american.edu American University generative AI policy ChatGPT academic integrity teaching guidelines
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned official American University OIT responsible-use guidance, an older interim AI guidance page, a Center for Faculty Excellence AI teaching page, and an Office of Academic Integrity PDF.
Robots.txt permits the OIT security guidance path. Direct curl from this worker received a Cloudflare challenge, so the successful snapshot used Firecrawl without bypassing the challenge locally.
Robots.txt permits public content outside /wp-admin/ and states Crawl-delay: 10. The CFE page and public OAI PDF were fetched through Firecrawl.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found current OIT responsible-use guidance, CFE teaching guidance, and an OAI academic-integrity PDF. The older interim OIT page was recorded as official but rejected as a duplicate/limited primary source because the current responsible-use page supersedes it for most claims.

artifacts.verificationNotes: Official OIT page, but it is interim guidance and overlaps with the current responsible-use guidance page. It was not used as primary evidence for this staged bundle.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: As the University works to finalize its guidance, the following interim guidelines offer students, faculty, and staff a framework for responsible use of AI tools.
artifacts.notes: The page is official and relevant, but it is older interim guidance; the current OIT responsible-use guidance is a stronger primary source.

stopConditions: Stop after official OIT, CFE, and OAI sources provide enough evidence for narrow AI policy claims.
Do not use SharePoint links requiring authentication or any unofficial summaries.

artifacts.errorReason: Direct curl from this worker received a Cloudflare managed challenge; no challenge bypass was attempted.

artifacts.sourceTitle: University Guidance on the Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI)
AI & Academic Integrity: OAI's Fall Guidance 2024

artifacts.claimText: American University publishes responsible-use guidance for AI tools that applies general principles of transparency, accountability, human oversight, data protection, privacy, teaching, research, staff, and student use.

citation.citationTitle: University Guidance on the Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI)
citation.publisher: American University Office of Information Technology
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: American University guidance says not to enter sensitive, Confidential, or Official Use data into an AI platform unless approved by a university official, and not to upload, input, or analyze university data in an AI tool that OIT has not approved.

citation.citationTitle: University Guidance on the Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI)
citation.publisher: American University Office of Information Technology
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: American University guidance places AI-use expectations at the course level: syllabi should state whether AI use is prohibited or permitted, and the CFE page says AU does not currently regulate faculty and student use of generative AI through a single university-wide rule.

citation.citationTitle: University Guidance on the Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI)

artifacts.claimText: American University guidance says students are responsible for course AI policies in syllabi, and misuse of AI or use without permission or disclosure may constitute an Academic Integrity Code violation.

artifacts.sourceTitle: University Guidance on the Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI)
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Be transparent about the use of AI in your research. Clearly track and, where appropriate, disclose when, how, and which AI tools (including versions) are used, whether for data analysis, content generation, or writing assistance.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: AU research guidance calls for transparent tracking and appropriate disclosure of AI use in research.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a source-status claim scoped to AU's official guidance page, not as a human-reviewed canonical public fact.
Approved because the claim closely follows the Data usage section and avoids expanding beyond OIT approval and university-official approval language.
Approved as a narrow course-level guidance claim supported by OIT and CFE language; it does not assert a single binding university-wide AI rule.
Approved because the claim uses 'may constitute' language and ties unauthorized or undisclosed use to the Academic Integrity Code without overstating all AI use as prohibited.
Approved as research guidance from the OIT responsible-use page; it avoids claiming IRB-specific or funder-specific requirements.

artifacts.title: American University AI Policy Staged Source Review
artifacts.summary: Staged review for American University found current official guidance on responsible AI use, course-level teaching expectations, data/privacy limits, academic-integrity implications, and research disclosure/accountability. Claims remain agent-reviewed candidates and are not canonical public facts.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Direct HTTP access to www.american.edu from this worker was blocked by a Cloudflare challenge; Firecrawl successfully retrieved the official OIT page and no challenge bypass was attempted.
Course-level and school-level AI policies may vary; this bundle does not assert that every American University course uses the same AI rule.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
